examples.text: وَنَهَانَا عَنْ سَبْعٍ، عَنْ خَاتَمِ الذَّهَبِ ـ أَوْ قَالَ حَلْقَةِ الذَّهَبِ ـ وَعَنْ لُبْسِ الْحَرِيرِ، وَٱلدِّيبَاجِ، وَٱلسُّنْدُسِ، وَٱلْمَيَاثِرِ.
examples.english: And he forbade us seven things: to wear golden signets – or golden rings –, to wear silk, silk brocade, fine silk or silk saddle-pillows.

etymology.text: From Middle Persian [script needed] (dypʾk' /⁠dēbāg⁠/, “brocade”). See Classical Persian دیبا (dēbā) for more. Doublet of دَوَاج (dawāj, “bed-quilt”).
